_comment: Stage 9a Weld: bevel TWO edges meeting at the bottom tip of a diamond (valence=2). Both edges meet at non-collinear ~120° angle; selCount=2 valence=2 non-collinear is the canonical 'weld' scenario from Blender's bevel terminology. Topology matches Blender exactly (9v/6f/same fv-dist); geometry close to within ~0.007 on a 0.1-width bevel — Blender keeps the two coincident BoundVerts as DUPLICATE vertices at one position (one per face), while vibe3d's aliasOf collapses them into a single vertex via the position-coincidence check. The collapsed vertex sits on the bev offset slightly differently from Blender's separate verts (Blender's are at the per-face computed positions which happen to be identical at machine precision; vibe3d takes one of them). Acceptable for this degenerate non-planar diamond — natural manifold meshes don't produce this configuration.
